deduplication.note: All 5 pod alerts deduplicated to 1 RR via OwnerResolver fingerprint. Fix for #1067 (PR #1068) confirmed: namespace no longer resolved as Namespace kind candidate.

rootCauseAnalysis.summary: Deployment api-gateway is CrashLoopBackOff because a bad kubectl patch swapped the volume's ConfigMap reference from gateway-config to gateway-config-bad. The replacement ConfigMap contains an invalid directive ('invalid_directive: true') that causes the application to abort at config parse time (exit code 1, 0s runtime). Rolling update kept 4 old pods alive (no full outage), but 3 new pods in ReplicaSet api-gateway-5b4ccdb7df are permanently stuck.
causalChain: Signal: KubePodCrashLooping — pods api-gateway-5b4ccdb7df-{ntt5j,pw924,vslv4} are in CrashLoopBackOff (6+ restarts, 5m back-off)
Why? Each container starts and exits within 0 seconds with exit code 1 and log: '[emerg] invalid directive found in /etc/demo-http-server/config.yaml — aborting'
Why? The config file mounted at /etc/demo-http-server/config.yaml contains 'invalid_directive: true', which the application rejects as an unknown directive
Why? The Deployment's volume references ConfigMap gateway-config-bad (instead of gateway-config), which was introduced by a kubectl patch at 2026-05-09T13:22:02Z

Root cause: ConfigMap gateway-config-bad contains an invalid configuration key ('invalid_directive: true') that was patched into the Deployment's volume spec, causing all pods from the new ReplicaSet to crash immediately on startup

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: The remediation target is Deployment/api-gateway because its spec.template.spec.volumes[0].configMap.name must be changed from 'gateway-config-bad' back to 'gateway-config'. This is the exact configuration field that is wrong.
dueDiligence.proportionality: Remediation targets the single Deployment whose volume spec contains the bad ConfigMap reference. This is the most specific, targeted fix.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: Deployment spec (both generations), both ReplicaSets, 3 crashing pods (logs, previous logs, events), both ConfigMaps (content compared), node assignments.
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced fully: CrashLoopBackOff → exit code 1 → config parse error → invalid directive in mounted config file → bad ConfigMap referenced in Deployment spec → kubectl patch introduced the bad reference.
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All claims are backed by tool evidence: PVC spec, pod logs (current and previous), ConfigMap content comparison, managedFields audit trail, Events (33 BackOff occurrences). No assumptions made.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history available for this target.
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: Considered and ruled out: OOMKill, image pull failure, node pressure, liveness probe misconfiguration, resource limits.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: 0.98: Starting at 1.0. -0.02 for not being able to identify the actor/system that issued the kubectl patch.

contributingFactors: ConfigMap gateway-config-bad contains 'invalid_directive: true' causing immediate crash on startup
kubectl patch at 2026-05-09T13:22:02Z swapped the Deployment volume's ConfigMap reference from gateway-config to gateway-config-bad
All 3 new pods crash within 0 seconds of starting — failure is at config parse time
Rolling update strategy (maxUnavailable: 25%) preserved 4 old healthy pods, preventing full outage
